label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won sprint in Dutch hometown stage after VAN POPPEL Danny delivered perfect leadout, TEUNISSEN Mike took red via time gaps at finish
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Fenix chased repeatedly for WELLENS but burned matches catching/releasing break multiple times before 60km mark, compromising sprint leadout.
Time bonuses split field enough that TEUNISSEN took race lead despite fourth place, suggesting intermediate sprint positioning matters more than assumed.
BORA-hansgrohe's VAN POPPEL provided winning leadout for rival team's BENNETT, indicating opportunistic sprint train alliances on domestique-light stages.

rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: negative: Alpecin-Fenix spent entire team chasing for him yet he failed to finish in top three, questioning stage-hunting value
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: won despite losing rhythm rubbing McLAY at 2km, showing resilience and bike-handling superior to pure positioning
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: pushed Bennett to photo finish without apparent leadout train, Trek's self-sufficient sprint approach viable on chaotic finishes
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Mike: positive: fourth place yielded race lead via bonus seconds, demonstrating consistent top-5 finishing more valuable than hunting stage wins
